title: Gynoxys woytkowskii
intro: Gynoxys woytkowskii is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family, native to the Andes mountains in South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to protect against certain diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Gynoxys woytkowskii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Gynoxys woytkowskii is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including arthritis, digestive issues, and stress-related disorders.
taxonomy: Gynoxys woytkowskii is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family, Asteraceae. It is native to the Andes Mountains in South America, specifically in Bolivia and Peru. The classification of Gynoxys woytkowskii is as follows: it is a member of the genus Gynoxys, which is a group of flowering plants that are commonly found in the Andes. The genus Gynoxys is further classified within the tribe Madieae, which is a subfamily of sunflowers. The exact classification of Gynoxys woytkowskii may be subject to further research and revision.
properties: Gynoxys woytkowskii, a plant species, is known for its potential therapeutic properties, particularly in traditional medicine.
